title: System and method of recognizing state of a product
abstract: There is provided a computerized method and system of recognizing state of a product, the product having a machine-readable visual code printed thereon, the method comprising: acquiring, by an image acquisition module, one or more images of the product wherein at least a first image of the one or more images includes the visual code printed on the product; detecting the visual code from the first image; analysing, in accordance with the detected visual code, at least a second image of the one or more images to provide an indication of the state of the product; and providing information to a user based on the indication.
claims:
Claims 1. A computerized method of recognizing state of a product, the product having a machine-readable visual code printed thereon, the method comprising: acquiring, by an image acquisition module, one or more images of the product wherein at least a first image of said one or more images includes the visual code printed on the product; detecting the visual code from the first image; analysing, in accordance with the detected visual code, at least a second image of said one or more images to provide an indication of the state of the product; and providing information to a user based on the indication. 2. The computerized method of claim 1, wherein the visual code is printed at a predetermined position relative to a given area of the product, said given area including an informative part of the product indicative of the state of the product, wherein said at least a second image includes the given area of the product, and wherein said analysing includes analysing at least the given area therein to provide an indication of the state of the product. 3. The computerized method of claim 2, wherein said second image is the first image. 4. The computerized method of claim 2, wherein said second image is a different image from the first image. 5. The computerized method of claim 4, wherein said second image is acquired after the detection of the visual code. 6. The computerized method of any of claims 1-5, wherein said visual code having data encoded therein, and wherein said detecting further comprises decoding the visual code to obtain the encoded data. 7. The computerized method of claim 6, wherein said analysing is performed in accordance with the encoded data of the visual code. 8. The computerized method of any of claims 1-7, wherein said analysing is performed in accordance with position of the detected visual code. 9. The computerized method of any of claims 2-9, wherein said analysing is performed by using an image recognition method. 10. The computerized method of any of claims 6-9, wherein the provided information being related to the encoded data. 11. The computerized method of any of claims 1-10, wherein said indication indicates the product or the package thereof is in one of the following states: opened, closed and consumed. 12. The computerized method of claim 6-11, wherein the encoded data includes at least one of the following: a digital coupon, a present, a lottery number, a URL and a pointer pointing to information residing in a remote server. 13. The computerized method of any of claims 6-12, wherein said provided information being related to the encoded data if the indication indicates that the product or the package thereof is in an opened or consumed state. 14. The computerized method of any of claims 1-12, wherein said provided information includes an instruction instructing the user to open or consume the product if the indication indicates that the product or the package thereof is in a closed state. 15. The computerized method of claim 14, wherein the instruction includes an opening option designated for the product, said opening option being different from one or more opening options of one or more other products in the same category. 16. The computerized method of any of claims 5-15, wherein the second image is acquired upon an automatic zoom-out operation being performed such that the second image capture a wider view of the product than the first image. 17. The computerized method of any of claims 5-15 further comprises, prior to the second image being acquired, providing an instruction of moving the image acquisition module in a direction to a user in order for the given area of the product to be captured by the second image. 18. The computerized method of any of claims 1-17 further comprises, prior to the providing information, verifying validity of the acquired one or more images based on a predefined three-dimensional model of the product; and wherein said providing information is performed upon the acquired one or more images being verified as valid. 19. The computerized method of claim 18 wherein said verifying comprises constructing a three-dimensional model for at least part of the product based on the acquired one or more images, and comparing the constructed three-dimensional model with the predefined three-dimensional model to determine the validity of the acquired one or more images. 20. The computerized method of any of claims 9-19, wherein the image recognition method is based on a machine-learning model. 21. The computerized method of any of claims 9-20, wherein the image recognition method includes calculating a classification for the acquired one or more images in accordance with a machine-learning model based on a training set of classified images. 22. The computerized method of any of claims 1-21, wherein the product is in form of a container, and the given area of the product includes a closure part of the product. 23. The computerized method of claim 22, wherein the closure part is one of the following: cap, cover, lid, and plug. 24. A computerized method of recognizing state of a product, the product having a machine-readable visual code printed thereon, the visual code having data encoded therein, the method comprising: acquiring, by an image acquisition module, one or more images of the product wherein at least a first image of said one or more images includes the visual code printed on the product; detecting the visual code from the first image to obtain the encoded data; analysing at least a second image of said one or more images to provide an indication of the state of the product; and providing information to a user based on the indication, said information being related to the encoded data. 25. A computerized system of recognizing state of a product, the product having a machine-readable visual code printed thereon, the system comprising: an image acquisition module configured to acquire one or more images of the product wherein at least a first image of said one or more images includes the visual code printed on the product; and a processing unit configured to: detect the visual code from the first image; analyse, in accordance with the detected visual code, at least a second image of said one or more images to provide an indication of the state of the product; and provide information to a user based on the indication. 26. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium tangibly embodying a program of instructions executable by a machine to recognize state of a product, the product having a machine -readable visual code printed thereon, comprising the steps of the following: acquiring, by an image acquisition module, one or more images of the product wherein at least a first image of said one or more images includes the visual code printed on the product; detecting the visual code from the first image; analysing, in accordance with the detected visual code, at least a second image of said one or more images to provide an indication of the state of the product; and providing information to a user based on the indication. 27. A computerized system of recognizing state of a product, the product having a machine-readable visual code printed thereon, the visual code having data encoded therein, the system comprising: 

 an image acquisition module configured to acquire one or more images of the product wherein at least a first image of said one or more images includes the visual code printed on the product; and a processing unit configured to: detect the visual code from the first image to obtain the encoded data; analyse at least a second image of said one or more images to provide an indication of the state of the product; and provide information to a user based on the indication, said information being related to the encoded data. 28. A non-transitory computer readable storage medium tangibly embodying a program of instructions executable by a machine to recognize state of a product, the product having a machine-readable visual code printed thereon, the visual code having data encoded therein, comprising the steps of the following: acquiring, by an image acquisition module, one or more images of the product wherein at least a first image of said one or more images includes the visual code printed on the product; detecting the visual code from the first image to obtain the encoded data; analysing at least a second image of said one or more images to provide an indication of the state of the product; and providing information to a user based on the indication, said information being related to the encoded data.
